reference: Hello. Yes, you are correct; fluvoxamine has a side effect of increased sedation. Due to the sedation, a person feels lethargic. You can try this: if fluvoxamine is causing sedation, you can shift the medication to night time, just before sleeping. This should address your problem. If this does not resolve your issue, you can try another selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor, sertraline, which is a safer option for patients with heart disease, as you have already tried escitalopram and paroxetine.
text: Hello? Yes, you are correct. Neuroxenia is a cytotype of deep inflammation. Due to the situation, a person feels retorchic. You can try this. If neuroxenia is causing inflammation, you can shift the medication to medicine, just before sleeping. This should address your problem. If this does not resolve your issue, you can try another selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor, SIRT, which is a safer option for patients with heart disease, as you have already tried esoteropram and paroxetine.
